channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: China reaches out to others as Trump layers on tariffs
item.description: China is reaching out to other nations as the U.S. layers on tariffs, in what appears to be an attempt by Beijing to form a united front to compel Washington to retreat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The week that Trump pushed the global economy to the brink with tariffs
item.description: It was one week after Donald Trump’s big tariff announcement when the president pulled back from the import taxes that he had imposed on many U.S. trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Seung Min Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the Dominican capital collapsed during a merengue concert, killing at least 184 people and injuring over 200 more.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 50 years later, Vietnam's battlefields draw retrospective veterans and other tourists
item.description: Hamburger Hill, Hue, the Ia Drang Valley, Khe Sanh: Some remember the Vietnam War battles from the headlines of the 1960s and 1970s, others from movies and history books.
item.dc:creator: David Rising And Hau Dinh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Panama and US lock in new security pact for Canal as China tensions simmer
item.description: Panama and the U.S. signed a new security pact on the canal, boosting defense ties as tensions with China over Latin American influence continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Wednesday at the Vatican during the royal couple's four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, DANICA KIRKA and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canadian PM Carney says negotiations with Trump on economic, security relationship to commence after election
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday that negotiations with President Trump on the U.S.-Canada economic and security relationships will start after the Canadian federal election.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The United States and China are locked in a faceoff over tariffs. No one wants to blink first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The tariff fight between the world's two largest economies spiraled into greater peril Wednesday as President Donald Trump tried to narrow his global trade war into a direct — and risky — faceoff with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US warns China intelligence targeting fired federal workers
item.description: The Trump administration is warning federal workers about efforts by Chinese intelligence to target current and former U.S. officials for recruitment.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Iran hit with new sanctions ahead of nuclear talks with US
item.description: The United States imposed new sanctions on Iran on Wednesday, days before the Islamic republic’s scheduled talks with Washington this weekend in Oman.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis meets privately with King Charles and Queen Camilla during his Vatican convalescence
item.description: Pope Francis met privately with King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the Vatican during the royal couple’s four-day state visit to Italy and on the occasion of their 20th wedding anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano And Colleen Barry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conservative leader Friedrich Merz secures German government coalition after months of political drift
item.description: German's center-right Christian Democratic Union and center-left Social Democrats have reached a deal to form a new German government under Friedrich Merz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump restoring some foreign aid programs
item.description: The Trump administration on Tuesday claimed foreign aid programs that were cut in error would be restored.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed off his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nicaragua’s crackdown on the Catholic Church is worsening, exiles and human rights advocates say
item.description: Preparations for popular, often daylong Lent and Holy Week processions are underway across Latin America — but not in Nicaragua. They’ve been largely banned for a second year, one of many concerns for the faithful in a country that human rights advocates, exiled priests and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's president again pledged Wednesday that his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States, going as far as dangling the prospect of direct American investment in the Islamic Republic if the countries can reach a deal.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Officials scramble to identify victims of Dominican club roof collapse that killed at least 184
item.description: The number of victims who died after a roof collapsed at an iconic nightclub has surged to 184 as dozens of people lingered outside the Dominican Republic’s forensic institute for news of their loved ones still missing more than a day after disaster struck.
item.dc:creator: Martín Adames Alcántara And Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US, Panama ‘taking back’ canal from ‘China's influence,’ says Hegseth
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth said the U.S. and Panama are strengthening security ties and "together" will "take back" the canal from the influence of China, which operates two major ports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Chinese men captured in Ukraine, Zelenskyy claims
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for action in the U.S. and Europe after claiming to capture two Chinese nationals fighting in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Death toll from North Macedonia nightclub fire rises to 61
item.description: SKOPJE, North Macedonia (AP) — The death toll from last month’s deadly nightclub fire in North Macedonia has risen to 61 after a second critically injured victim died while receiving treatment in Lithuania, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Duke basketball star could be deported under Trump visa policy
item.description: The State Department announced Saturday that visas of South Sudanese passport holders would be revoked.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills a journalist in a media tent outside a Gaza hospital
item.description: Israel struck tents outside two major hospitals in the Gaza Strip overnight, killing at least two people, including a local reporter, and wounding nine, including six journalists, Palestinian medics said. Separate strikes killed at least 15 others across the Gaza Strip, according to hospitals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu to meet at White House as Israel seeks tariff relief, discussions on Iran, Gaza hostages
item.description: President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu set for White House meeting to discuss ending U.S. tariffs on Israel, the war in Gaza, the release of hostages and Iran's nuclear threat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN global comms arm under fire for anti-Israel bias as critics call for reforms
item.description: The U.N.'s Department of Global Communications is under fire for a one-sided view of the Israel-Palestinian conflict, with critics saying it often sides with Palestinians at the expense of other conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JD Vance's warning on Europe's future shines spotlight on continent's growing list of problems
item.description: Many countries in Europe are struggling to come to terms with mass migration, rising crime and weak economies. But some nations have taken action and are seeing hopeful results.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: The Who... not too old to rock
item.description: Roger Daltrey and Pete Townshend lit up London in a rare, fiery performance for Teenage Cancer Trust — decades older, still unmistakably The Who.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN official reappointed despite accusations of antisemitism
item.description: Allegations of antisemitism and the condemnation of several nations did not stop Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese from securing another three-year term in her position.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation
